title: Multi-peer pinning is unaffordable on nRF52840: constant-time all-peers Schnorr verify keeps radio un-armed
description: peer_try_all_pubkeys verifies EVERY demodulated frame against ALL pinned peers (constant-time, no early exit even after a match). On the 64 MHz nRF52840 each Schnorr-48 verify is slow, and the LoRa RX thread does not re-arm lora_recv() during processing - so every ambient frame makes the radio deaf for N_peers verifies. Measured on the bench 2026-07-17: T-Echo with 1 pinned peer in 3-node RF = 6/14 CoAP round trips; with 2 pinned peers = 0/24 (both directions healthy at L2, responses simply never demodulated). Gateway (ESP32-S3 @ 240 MHz) handles 2 peers fine. Related: 3 ACTIVE nodes at SF10 exceed the ALOHA airtime budget (~45% offered load with the deaf T1000-E retransmitting every cycle + its responses) - see conf comments in lichen/apps/*/boards/. Options: (a) allow early-exit iteration in dev mode (timing side-channel is a non-issue for INSECURE bench provisioning), (b) cheap pre-filter before verify (e.g. try the peer whose window/last-seen matches first - still verify-all on miss), (c) re-arm the radio before verification (queue frames, verify out of RX path - needs RAM), (d) CSMA/backoff + duty limits for airtime. The multi-peer dev provisioning feature itself works and stays; the confs pin single peers on nRF52840 until this is addressed.
close_reason: HARDWARE VALIDATED 2026-07-17. Re-pinned two peers on the T-Echo (gateway ee45..f281 first as the CoAP destination, then the sibling T1000-E 8a1a..2cab), rebuilt + reflashed. With the dev-mode early-exit fix the round trip now sustains 6/9 (67%) over a 2-min window — versus 0/24 (0%) measured with two peers BEFORE the fix. The verify-cost starvation is resolved: matched frames (gateway responses) exit the peer loop after the first Schnorr verify instead of always running all N with the radio un-armed. Ordering gateway-first means its responses exit on iteration 1. The remaining ~33% loss is the separate 3-node SF10 airtime contention (deaf T1000-E free-running + retransmits), a known limitation noted in this bead, not verify cost. link_crypto + ping_l2 unchanged; secure (non-dev) builds keep the constant-time all-peers scan.
